2604.07781.title: Toward Generalizable Graph Learning for 3D Engineering AI: Explainable Workflows for CAE Mode Shape Classification and CFD Field Prediction
2604.07781.paper_summary: Automotive engineering development increasingly relies on heterogeneous 3D data, including finite element (FE) models, body-in-white (BiW) representations, CAD geometry, and CFD meshes. At the same time, engineering teams face growing pressure to shorten development cycles, improve performance and accelerate innovation. Although artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly explored in this domain, many current methods remain task-specific, difficult to interpret, and hard to reuse across development stages. This paper presents a practical graph learning framework for 3D engineering AI, in which heterogeneous engineering assets are converted into physics-aware graph representations and processed by Graph Neural Networks (GNNs). The framework is designed to support both classification and prediction tasks. The framework is validated on two automotive applications: CAE vibration mode shape classification and CFD aerodynamic field prediction. For CAE vibration mode classification, a region-aware BiW graph supports explainable mode classification across vehicle and FE variants under label scarcity. For CFD aerodynamic field prediction, a physics-informed surrogate predicts pressure and wall shear stress (WSS) across aerodynamic body shape variants, while symmetry preserving down sampling retains accuracy with lower computational cost. The framework also outlines data generation guidance that can help engineers identify which additional simulations or labels are valuable to collect next. These results demonstrate a practical and reusable engineering AI workflow for more trustworthy CAE and CFD decision support.
2604.07781.paper_summary_zh: 汽車工程的發展越來越依賴於異質的 3D 數據，包括有限元素 (FE) 模型、白車身 (BiW) 表示、CAD 幾何形狀和 CFD 網格。<lb/>同時，工程團隊面臨著縮短開發周期、提高性能和加速創新的日益壓力。<lb/>儘管人工智慧 (AI) 在這一領域的探索越來越多，但許多當前的方法仍然是特定任務的、難以解釋的，並且在不同開發階段之間難以重用。<lb/>本文提出了一種實用的圖學習框架，用於 3D 工程 AI，其中異質工程資產被轉換為具有物理感知的圖表示，並由圖神經網絡 (GNNs) 處理。<lb/>該框架旨在支持分類和預測任務。<lb/>該框架在兩個汽車應用上進行了驗證：CAE 振動模式形狀分類和 CFD 氣動場預測。<lb/>對於 CAE 振動模式分類，一個區域感知的 BiW 圖支持在標籤稀缺的情況下進行可解釋的模式分類，涵蓋了車輛和 FE 變體。<lb/>對於 CFD 氣動場預測，一個物理知識驅動的替代模型預測了氣動體形狀變體的壓力和壁面剪切應力 (WSS)，同時保持對稱的下採樣在降低計算成本的同時保持準確性。<lb/>該框架還概述了數據生成指導，幫助工程師識別哪些額外的模擬或標籤是值得收集的。<lb/>這些結果展示了一個實用且可重用的工程 AI 工作流程，為更可靠的 CAE 和 CFD 決策支持提供了幫助。
2604.07781.author: Tong Duy Son et.al.
2604.07781.authors: Tong Duy Son, Kohta Sugiura, Marc Brughmans, Andrey Hense, Zhihao Liu, Amirthalakshmi Veeraraghavan, Ajinkya Bhave, Jay Masters, Paolo di Carlo, Theo Geluk
